front: What is the primary purpose of Garbage Collection (GC) in Java?
back: To automatically manage memory by freeing up heap space occupied by objects that are no longer in use by the application, preventing memory leaks.

front: How does an object become eligible for garbage collection in Java?
back: An object becomes eligible when there are no more live references to it. This happens when references are set to null, reassigned, or when the reference goes out of scope.

front: What is the finalize() method and how is it related to GC?
back: It's a method called by the garbage collector on an object once, just before the object is reclaimed. It's not recommended for critical resource cleanup due to its unpredictable execution timing.

front: What is the Java Heap memory divided into generational spaces (Young & Old Generation), and what is the purpose?
back: Most objects die young. The heap is divided to optimize GC. The Young Generation (Eden, S0, S1) is for new objects and is collected frequently (Minor GC). The Old Generation is for long-lived objects and is collected less often (Major GC).

front: Can a Java programmer force the Garbage Collector to run?
back: No. You can only suggest it to the JVM using System.gc() or Runtime.getRuntime().gc(), but there is no guarantee the GC will run immediately. The JVM ultimately decides.
